target.name: Cognition
target.founder: Scott Wu, Steven Hao, Walden Yan

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $300.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: OpenAI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $300B+

private.name: Thinking Machines Lab
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $12B

private.name: Sakana AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Cognition is a Seed-stage AI & Software company founded by Scott Wu, Steven Hao, and Walden Yan that has raised $175M+. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine private companies and one public comparable (Palantir), including OpenAI, Thinking Machines Lab, Sakana AI, and xAI.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression modeling, using the $175M raised as a proxy for a $5M base valuation and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, implies a valuation of $12M. The peer-median approach compares Cognition to three same-sector, same-stage peers. The median valuation across OpenAI ($300B), Thinking Machines Lab ($12B), and Sakana AI ($1B) is $12.0B, though the range spans $1.0B to $300.0B, reflecting material dispersion in the AI & Software seed-stage comp set.

The triangulation produces a median implied valuation of $6.0B, with a low bound of $10M and a high bound of $13.8B. The wide range reflects the extreme heterogeneity in the peer set—particularly the outsized influence of OpenAI—and limited visibility into how capital deployment and product traction map to stage advancement in frontier AI. Key uncertainties center on appropriate peer selection for an early-stage AI company and whether stage-progression mechanics apply in this sector's non-traditional funding patterns.
